import re

def test_issue_3764_loc_tests_split_and_parametrized__a1():
    """
    Verify that the issue #3764 regression test was moved out of `test_loc`
    into its own test cases and uses `@pytest.mark.parametrize("left, right", ...)`.

    This is a structural/functional test of the test suite organization change requested
    in the code review comment.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/modin/pandas/test/dataframe/test_indexing.py').read()
    assert '@pytest.mark.parametrize("left, right", [(2, 1), (6, 1), (lambda df: 70, 1), (90, 70)])' in source, "Expected issue #3764 row-insertion test to be split into a separate test case and parametrized with @pytest.mark.parametrize('left, right', ...)."

def test__getitem_bool_single_row_dataframe():

    # https://github.com/modin-project/modin/issues/4845

# This is a very subtle bug that comes from:
# https://github.com/modin-project/modin/issues/4945
def test_lazy_eval_index():
    modin_df, pandas_df = create_test_dfs({"col0": [0, 1]})

    def func(df):
        df_copy = df[df["col0"] < 6].copy()
        # The problem here is that the index is not copied over so it needs
        # to get recomputed at some point. Our implementation of __setitem__
        # requires us to build a mask and insert the value from the right
        # handside into the new DataFrame. However, it's possible that we
        # won't have any new partitions, so we will end up computing an empty
        # index.
        df_copy["col0"] = df_copy["col0"].apply(lambda x: x + 1)
        return df_copy

    eval_general(modin_df, pandas_df, func)

import re

def test_setitem_with_new_columns_shape_check_refactored_to_tuple_compare__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/modin/pandas/indexing.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'def _setitem_with_new_columns' in source, 'Expected indexing helper method `_setitem_with_new_columns` to exist in /workspace/modin/pandas/indexing.py.'

def test_setitem_with_new_columns_shape_check_refactored_to_tuple_compare__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/modin/pandas/indexing.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('def _setitem_with_new_columns[\\s\\S]*?if\\s+len\\(item\\.shape\\)\\s*==\\s*1\\s*:[\\s\\S]*?else\\s*:\\s*(?P<branch>[\\s\\S]*?)\\n\\s*exist_items\\s*=', source)
    assert m is not None, 'Could not locate the shape-validation logic in `_setitem_with_new_columns` (expected an `if len(item.shape) == 1: ... else: ...` structure).'

def test_setitem_with_new_columns_shape_check_refactored_to_tuple_compare__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/modin/pandas/indexing.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('def _setitem_with_new_columns[\\s\\S]*?if\\s+len\\(item\\.shape\\)\\s*==\\s*1\\s*:[\\s\\S]*?else\\s*:\\s*(?P<branch>[\\s\\S]*?)\\n\\s*exist_items\\s*=', source)
    branch = m.group('branch')
    assert 'item.shape !=' in branch, 'Expected the 2D-shape validation to compare `item.shape` against a tuple like `(len(self.qc.index), len(col_loc))`, but it did not. This indicates the pre-refactor style is still present.'

def test_setitem_with_new_columns_shape_check_refactored_to_tuple_compare__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/modin/pandas/indexing.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('def _setitem_with_new_columns[\\s\\S]*?if\\s+len\\(item\\.shape\\)\\s*==\\s*1\\s*:[\\s\\S]*?else\\s*:\\s*(?P<branch>[\\s\\S]*?)\\n\\s*exist_items\\s*=', source)
    branch = m.group('branch')
    assert 'item.shape[0]' not in branch and 'item.shape[1]' not in branch, 'Expected the refactored tuple-based shape check (no `item.shape[0]`/`item.shape[1]`), but found indexed shape access in the 2D-shape validation branch.'

def test_loc_insert_row_function_moved_out_of_loop_and_parametrized__a1():

    defined inside a `for left, right in ...` loop; instead it should be defined once
    and the test should be parameterized (as done in the "after" code).
